title: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus
intro: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus, also known as the Round Conk Oak, is a species of plant in the beech family that is native to China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus include decoctions and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus is classified within the family Fagaceae, which includes other oak and beech species. This genus, Lithocarpus, is a group of evergreen trees native to Asia. The classification of Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It belongs to the order Fagales and the class Magnoliopsida. At the species level, Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus is the only species within its genus.

benefits: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus, also known as the Taiwan stone oak, is a species of tree that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The tree's bark and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Additionally, the tree's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various infections. The health benefits of Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus may also include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and to confirm its effectiveness as a treatment for various conditions.

parts: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus is a species of plant in the beech family, native to China and Southeast Asia.  Its medicinal properties are attributed to the use of various parts of the plant for treating health issues.  The bark of Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus has been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions.  Additionally, the leaves and roots of the plant are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.  The plant's medicinal parts are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
preparations: Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus, commonly known as the Philippine tanbark oak, is a plant that has been used in traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations made from the plant are primarily used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are typically used in these preparations. The active compounds found in Lithocarpus sphaerocarpus are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and optimal dosages.
